examples.title: Algorithm: Frame Allocation Schemes
examples.description: Pseudocode demonstrating Equal, Proportional, and Priority frame allocation algorithms
examples.code:
ALGORITHM: Fixed Allocation (Equal)
INPUT: total_frames, num_processes
OUTPUT: frames_per_process

BEGIN
 frames_per_process = total_frames / num_processes
 RETURN frames_per_process
END

---

ALGORITHM: Proportional Allocation
INPUT: processes[] with sizes, total_frames
OUTPUT: allocation[] for each process

BEGIN
 total_size = 0
 FOR each p in processes DO
 total_size = total_size + p.size
 END FOR

 FOR each p in processes DO
 allocation[p.id] = (p.size / total_size) * total_frames
 END FOR

 RETURN allocation
END

---

ALGORITHM: Priority Allocation
INPUT: processes[] with priorities and sizes, total_frames
OUTPUT: allocation[] for each process

BEGIN
 total_priority = 0
 FOR each p in processes DO
 total_priority = total_priority + p.priority
 END FOR

 FOR each p in processes DO
 allocation[p.id] = (p.priority / total_priority) * total_frames
 END FOR

 RETURN allocation
END

---

ALGORITHM: Working Set Model
INPUT: process, window_size_delta, page_reference_log
OUTPUT: working_set_size

BEGIN
 working_set = EMPTY_SET
 current_time = NOW

 FOR each reference IN page_reference_log DO
 IF (current_time - reference.time) <= window_size_delta THEN
 ADD reference.page TO working_set
 END IF
 END FOR

 working_set_size = SIZE(working_set)
 RETURN working_set_size
END
examples.explanation: The pseudocode shows four allocation algorithms: (1) Equal Allocation divides frames evenly among processes, (2) Proportional Allocation allocates frames based on process size, (3) Priority Allocation gives more frames to higher priority processes, and (4) Working Set Model tracks the set of pages referenced in the recent time window to prevent thrashing.

examples.title: Python: Frame Allocation Simulator
examples.description: Complete Python implementation of frame allocation schemes including proportional allocation and working set tracking
examples.code:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Frame Allocation Simulator
Implements Equal, Proportional, and Priority allocation schemes
"""

class Process:
 def __init__(self, pid, size, priority=1):
 self.pid = pid
 self.size = size # in pages
 self.priority = priority
 self.frames_allocated = 0
 self.page_history = [] # Track page references

 def __repr__(self):
 return f"P{self.pid}(size={self.size}, priority={self.priority})"


def equal_allocation(total_frames, processes):
 """
 Divide frames equally among all processes
 Simple but may be inefficient for varying process sizes
 """
 n = len(processes)
 if n == 0:
 return {}
 
 frames_per_process = total_frames // n
 remainder = total_frames % n
 
 allocation = {}
 for i, p in enumerate(processes):
 # Distribute remainder frames to first few processes
 extra = 1 if i < remainder else 0
 allocation[p.pid] = frames_per_process + extra
 p.frames_allocated = allocation[p.pid]
 
 return allocation


def proportional_allocation(total_frames, processes):
 """
 Allocate frames proportional to process size
 More fair than equal allocation
 """
 if not processes:
 return {}
 
 total_size = sum(p.size for p in processes)
 if total_size == 0:
 return equal_allocation(total_frames, processes)
 
 allocation = {}
 for p in processes:
 # Calculate proportional share
 share = (p.size / total_size) * total_frames
 allocation[p.pid] = int(share)
 p.frames_allocated = allocation[p.pid]
 
 # Handle rounding - give remaining frames to largest process
 allocated_sum = sum(allocation.values())
 remainder = total_frames - allocated_sum
 if remainder > 0:
 largest = max(processes, key=lambda p: p.size)
 allocation[largest.pid] += remainder
 largest.frames_allocated += remainder
 
 return allocation


def priority_allocation(total_frames, processes):
 """
 Allocate frames based on process priority
 Higher priority processes get more frames
 """
 if not processes:
 return {}
 
 total_priority = sum(p.priority for p in processes)
 if total_priority == 0:
 return equal_allocation(total_frames, processes)
 
 allocation = {}
 for p in processes:
 share = (p.priority / total_priority) * total_frames
 allocation[p.pid] = int(share)
 p.frames_allocated = allocation[p.pid]
 
 # Handle rounding
 allocated_sum = sum(allocation.values())
 remainder = total_frames - allocated_sum
 if remainder > 0:
 highest = max(processes, key=lambda p: p.priority)
 allocation[highest.pid] += remainder
 highest.frames_allocated += remainder
 
 return allocation


def working_set_model(process, delta, current_time):
 """
 Calculate working set: pages referenced in last delta time units
 Helps determine minimum frames needed to prevent thrashing
 """
 # Filter references within the window
 recent_refs = [
 ref for ref in process.page_history
 if current_time - ref['time'] <= delta
 ]
 
 # Get unique pages in the working set
 working_set = set(ref['page'] for ref in recent_refs)
 
 return working_set, len(working_set)


# Demonstration
if __name__ == "__main__":
 # Create sample processes
 processes = [
 Process(1, 100, priority=3), # Large, high priority
 Process(2, 50, priority=2), # Medium, medium priority
 Process(3, 25, priority=1), # Small, low priority
 ]
 
 total_frames = 60
 
 print("=" * 50)
 print("FRAME ALLOCATION SIMULATOR")
 print("=" * 50)
 print(f"Total Available Frames: {total_frames}")
 print(f"Number of Processes: {len(processes)}")
 print("-" * 50)
 
 # Test Equal Allocation
 print("\n1. EQUAL ALLOCATION")
 equal_result = equal_allocation(total_frames, processes.copy())
 for pid, frames in equal_result.items():
 print(f" Process {pid}: {frames} frames")
 
 # Test Proportional Allocation
 print("\n2. PROPORTIONAL ALLOCATION")
 proportional_result = proportional_allocation(total_frames, processes.copy())
 for pid, frames in proportional_result.items():
 print(f" Process {pid}: {frames} frames")
 
 # Test Priority Allocation
 print("\n3. PRIORITY ALLOCATION")
 priority_result = priority_allocation(total_frames, processes.copy())
 for pid, frames in priority_result.items():
 print(f" Process {pid}: {frames} frames")
 
 # Demonstrate Working Set Model
 print("\n4. WORKING SET MODEL")
 demo_process = Process(1, 100)
 # Simulate page references with timestamps
 demo_process.page_history = [
 {'page': 1, 'time': 1},
 {'page': 2, 'time': 2},
 {'page': 3, 'time': 3},
 {'page': 1, 'time': 4},
 {'page': 4, 'time': 10}, # Outside window if delta=5
 ]
 
 ws, ws_size = working_set_model(demo_process, delta=5, current_time=7)
 print(f" Working Set (pages): {ws}")
 print(f" Working Set Size: {ws_size} frames needed")
examples.explanation: This Python implementation demonstrates three key frame allocation schemes: (1) Equal Allocation divides frames evenly, (2) Proportional Allocation uses process size for distribution, and (3) Priority Allocation uses process priority. It also includes a Working Set Model that tracks recently referenced pages to determine minimum frame requirements.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing allocations and process data

examples.title: C: Frame Allocation Calculator
examples.description: C implementation showing fixed and proportional allocation with priority support
examples.explanation: This C implementation provides three frame allocation algorithms: (1) Equal Allocation divides available frames evenly among processes, (2) Proportional Allocation allocates based on process size using floating-point calculations, and (3) Priority Allocation distributes frames according to process priority. The code demonstrates how the OS calculates frame distribution and handles remainder frames.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for all allocation algorithms where n is the number of processes
